name: Kachumber Salad
preparation: Dice cucumber, tomato, and onion into small uniform pieces.
Finely chop green chillies and fresh coriander.
Combine all vegetables in a mixing bowl.
Add lemon juice, salt, black pepper, and roasted cumin powder.
Toss gently to mix evenly.
Serve immediately as a side dish.
notes: A classic Indian side salad served with most meals. Best eaten fresh.

name: Onion Tomato Kachumber
ingredients: 1 large onion, thinly sliced into rings
preparation: Slice onion into thin rings and separate them.
Cut tomatoes into thin round slices.
Arrange onion rings and tomato slices on a plate.
Sprinkle salt, red chilli powder, and lemon juice.
Garnish with slit green chilli and fresh coriander.
Serve fresh alongside dal-rice or roti.
notes: A tangy and spicy everyday accompaniment found in every Indian household.

name: Cucumber Kachumber
preparation: Peel and dice cucumber into small pieces.
Finely chop onion, green chilli, and coriander.
Mix cucumber and onion in a bowl.
Add lemon juice, salt, and chaat masala.
Toss well and garnish with coriander.
Serve chilled for best taste.
notes: Refreshing cucumber-based kachumber perfect for hot summer days.

name: Carrot Kachumber
preparation: Grate carrots and place in a mixing bowl.
Finely chop onion, green chilli, and coriander.
Heat oil in a small pan, add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
Pour the tempering over grated carrots.
Add onion, green chilli, lemon juice, and salt.
Mix well and garnish with coriander.
notes: A crunchy carrot kachumber with a South Indian-style tempering.

name: Cucumber Raita
preparation: Grate cucumber and squeeze out excess water.
Whisk curd until smooth in a bowl.
Add grated cucumber to the curd.
Season with roasted cumin powder, salt, and black pepper.
Mix gently and sprinkle chaat masala on top.
Garnish with coriander and serve chilled.
notes: A cooling raita that pairs perfectly with biryani and spicy dishes.

name: Boondi Raita
preparation: Soak boondi in warm water for 5 minutes, then drain and squeeze gently.
Whisk curd until smooth and creamy.
Add softened boondi to the curd.
Season with roasted cumin powder, salt, and red chilli powder.
Mix well and sprinkle chaat masala on top.
Garnish with coriander and refrigerate for 15 minutes before serving.
notes: A popular raita with soft boondi pearls, commonly served at Indian feasts.

name: Onion Raita
preparation: Finely chop onion and green chilli.
Whisk curd until smooth.
Add chopped onion and green chilli to the curd.
Season with roasted cumin powder, salt, and black pepper.
Mix gently and garnish with fresh mint.
Serve chilled alongside biryani or pulao.
notes: A simple onion raita that adds a fresh crunch to any Indian meal.

name: Tomato Raita
ingredients: 1 medium tomato, deseeded and finely chopped
preparation: Deseed tomato and chop into fine pieces.
Whisk curd until smooth and lump-free.
Add chopped tomato to the curd.
Season with roasted cumin powder, salt, sugar, and red chilli powder.
Mix gently to combine.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve chilled.
notes: A mildly tangy raita with fresh tomatoes, great with parathas.

name: Mint Raita
preparation: Blend mint leaves and green chilli with 2 tbsp water to a fine paste.
Whisk curd until smooth.
Add mint paste to the curd and mix well.
Season with roasted cumin powder, salt, sugar, and black salt.
Stir to combine all flavors.
Chill for 15 minutes before serving.
notes: A vibrant green raita with refreshing mint flavor, perfect with kebabs.

name: Pineapple Raita
preparation: Chop pineapple into small pieces.
Whisk curd until smooth and creamy.
Add pineapple pieces to the curd.
Season with roasted cumin powder, salt, black pepper, and sugar.
Mix gently to combine.
Garnish with coriander and serve chilled.
notes: A sweet and tangy raita with tropical pineapple, great with spicy curries.

name: Pomegranate Raita
preparation: Whisk curd until smooth.
Add pomegranate arils to the curd.
Season with roasted cumin powder, salt, sugar, and black salt.
Mix gently so pomegranate arils stay whole.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Refrigerate and serve chilled.
notes: A beautiful ruby-red raita with juicy pomegranate seeds.

name: Beetroot Raita
preparation: Boil beetroot until tender, then peel and grate.
Whisk curd until smooth.
Add grated beetroot to the curd and mix.
Season with roasted cumin powder, salt, sugar, and chaat masala.
Stir gently until curd turns a vibrant pink.
Garnish with coriander and serve chilled.
notes: A naturally pink-colored raita with earthy beetroot flavor.

name: Bottle Gourd Raita
ingredients: 1/2 cup bottle gourd (lauki), grated and cooked
preparation: Grate bottle gourd and cook in a pan until soft. Let cool.
Whisk curd until smooth.
Add cooled grated bottle gourd to the curd.
Season with roasted cumin powder, salt, black pepper, and mint powder.
Mix well and garnish with coriander.
Serve chilled as a healthy side.
notes: A light and nutritious raita with mild-flavored bottle gourd.

name: Sprout Salad
preparation: Steam or boil mixed sprouts until tender yet crunchy, about 5 minutes.
Let sprouts cool to room temperature.
Add chopped onion, tomato, cucumber, and green chilli.
Drizzle with lemon juice and season with chaat masala and salt.
Toss everything together gently.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve.
notes: A protein-rich sprout salad, ideal as a healthy breakfast or snack.

name: Moong Sprout Salad Indian
preparation: Steam moong sprouts for 5-7 minutes until tender but still crunchy.
Drain and let cool.
Add chopped onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, and turmeric.
Toss well to coat evenly.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve.
notes: Moong sprouts are rich in protein and easy to digest. Perfect for weight loss.

name: Mixed Sprout Salad
preparation: Steam all sprouts together for 5-7 minutes.
Drain and let them cool completely.
Mix sprouts with onion, tomato, cucumber, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala, and salt.
Toss gently to combine all ingredients.
Garnish with coriander and serve fresh.
notes: A tri-sprout salad loaded with protein and dietary fiber.

name: Chana Sprout Salad
preparation: Steam chana sprouts for 8-10 minutes until tender.
Let them cool to room temperature.
Add chopped onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, and red chilli powder.
Mix everything together well.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Chana sprouts are high in protein and give a satisfying chew.

name: Matki Sprout Salad
preparation: Steam matki sprouts for 5-7 minutes until just tender.
Drain and cool.
Add onion, tomato, grated coconut, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, salt, and turmeric.
Toss well to combine.
Garnish with coriander. Popular in Maharashtrian cuisine.
notes: A Maharashtrian-style sprout salad with coconut for extra flavor.

name: Chana Salad
preparation: Boil chickpeas until soft or use canned chickpeas, drained.
Add chopped onion, tomato, cucumber, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, and red chilli powder.
Toss all ingredients together well.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve at room temperature or chilled.
notes: A filling chickpea salad packed with plant protein.

name: Chickpea Chaat Salad
preparation: Boil chickpeas and potato until tender. Dice potato.
Combine chickpeas, potato, onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Drizzle tamarind chutney and green chutney over the mixture.
Add chaat masala and salt, toss well.
Top with sev and fresh coriander.
Serve immediately for best crunch.
notes: A street food-style chaat salad with sweet and tangy chutneys.

name: Kabuli Chana Salad
preparation: Boil kabuli chana until soft, drain and cool.
Dice onion, tomato, cucumber, and bell pepper.
Combine all vegetables with chickpeas in a bowl.
Add lemon juice, cumin powder, salt, and black pepper.
Toss well to mix all flavors.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Kabuli chana has a buttery texture that makes this salad satisfying.

name: Black Chana Salad
preparation: Soak black chana overnight and pressure cook until tender.
Drain and let cool.
Add chopped onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, red chilli powder, and amchur.
Mix well to coat the chana with spices.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Black chana is more nutritious than white and has a nuttier flavor.

name: Kosambari
ingredients: 1/2 cup split moong dal, soaked 30 minutes
preparation: Soak split moong dal in water for 30 minutes, then drain.
Chop cucumber finely and grate carrot and coconut.
Mix dal, cucumber, carrot, coconut, and green chilli.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves. Let splutter.
Pour tempering over the salad.
Add lemon juice and salt, toss and serve.
notes: A traditional South Indian lentil salad served during festivals and poojas.

name: Hesaru Bele Kosambari
preparation: Soak moong dal for 30 minutes and drain well.
Finely chop cucumber and grate carrot.
Combine soaked dal, cucumber, carrot, coconut, and green chilli.
Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Pour tempering over the salad and add lemon juice and salt.
Mix gently and serve at room temperature.
notes: Hesaru Bele is Kannada for moong dal. A Karnataka festival staple.

name: Cucumber Kosambari
ingredients: 1/2 cup split chana dal, soaked 1 hour
preparation: Soak chana dal for 1 hour and drain thoroughly.
Dice cucumber finely.
Mix chana dal, cucumber, grated coconut, and green chilli.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, asafoetida, and curry leaves.
Pour tempering over the salad.
Add lemon juice and salt, mix well and serve.
notes: Cucumber-based kosambari using chana dal for a different texture.

name: Carrot Kosambari
preparation: Soak moong dal for 30 minutes and drain.
Grate carrots and fresh coconut.
Combine dal, carrot, coconut, and green chilli.
Heat oil and add mustard seeds and curry leaves until they splutter.
Pour tempering over the salad.
Add lemon juice and salt, toss and serve immediately.
notes: Carrot adds natural sweetness to this traditional kosambari.

name: Raw Mango Kosambari
preparation: Soak moong dal for 30 minutes, drain well.
Grate raw mango and coconut.
Mix dal, raw mango, coconut, and green chilli.
Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Pour tempering over the mixture.
Add salt and sugar, toss well and serve.
notes: A tangy summer kosambari with seasonal raw mango.

name: Beetroot Salad Indian
preparation: Boil beetroot until tender, peel and dice into cubes.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves until they splutter.
Combine diced beetroot, onion, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour the tempering over the salad.
Add lemon juice and salt, toss gently.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A vibrant South Indian-style beetroot salad with coconut.

name: Beetroot Poriyal Salad
preparation: Grate beetroot finely.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, red chillies, and curry leaves.
When mustard seeds splutter, add grated beetroot.
Sprinkle turmeric and salt, stir-fry for 3-4 minutes.
Remove from heat and add grated coconut.
Mix well. Serve warm or at room temperature as a salad-side.
notes: A poriyal-style salad from Tamil Nadu, lightly cooked for extra nutrition.

name: Beetroot Raita Salad
preparation: Boil beetroot, peel, and grate finely.
Whisk curd until smooth and creamy.
Mix in the grated beetroot.
Add roasted cumin powder, salt, sugar, and black pepper.
Stir well until curd turns pink.
Garnish with coriander and serve chilled.
notes: A colorful raita-salad hybrid with the earthy sweetness of beetroot.

name: Beetroot Carrot Salad
preparation: Grate beetroot and carrot.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves until they splutter.
Combine grated beetroot, carrot, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour tempering over the salad.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Toss well and serve fresh.
notes: A colorful duo salad with the nutrition of beetroot and carrot.

name: Carrot Peanut Salad
preparation: Grate carrots and keep aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
In a bowl, combine grated carrots, roasted peanuts, and green chilli.
Pour the tempering over the salad.
Add lemon juice and salt, toss well.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Peanuts add crunch and protein to this simple carrot salad.

name: Carrot Raisin Salad Indian
preparation: Grate carrots finely.
Soak raisins in warm water for 10 minutes, drain.
Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine carrots, raisins, and peanuts in a bowl.
Add tempering, lemon juice, salt, and sugar.
Mix well, garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A sweet and savory combination with raisins and peanuts.

name: Grated Carrot Salad
preparation: Grate fresh carrots.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves until they pop.
Combine grated carrot, coconut, and green chilli in a bowl.
Pour tempering over the salad.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Toss gently and serve immediately.
notes: A minimalist South Indian carrot salad with just coconut and tempering.

name: Carrot Coconut Salad
preparation: Grate carrots and coconut.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, and curry leaves.
When dal turns golden, pour tempering over the carrots.
Add coconut, green chilli, lemon juice, and salt.
Mix everything well.
Serve as a side with rice and sambar.
notes: Fresh coconut makes this salad rich and aromatic.

name: Green Mango Salad
preparation: Peel raw mango and cut into thin julienne strips.
Slice onion thinly.
Combine mango strips and onion in a bowl.
Add salt, red chilli powder, and sugar.
Toss well to coat all pieces.
Garnish with slit green chilli and coriander.
notes: A tangy summer salad best enjoyed during mango season.

name: Raw Mango Chutney Salad
preparation: Grate raw mango.
Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves.
In a bowl, combine grated mango, jaggery, red chilli powder, and salt.
Add the tempering and crushed peanuts.
Mix until jaggery starts to melt and coat the mango.
Serve as a sweet-tangy-spicy salad.
notes: A Maharashtrian-style chutney-salad with jaggery and raw mango.

name: Keri Ka Salad
preparation: Peel and dice raw mango into small cubes.
Chop onion, green chilli, and coriander.
Combine mango cubes and onion in a bowl.
Add salt, chaat masala, red chilli powder, black salt, and lemon juice.
Toss well to combine flavors.
Garnish with coriander. Serve fresh.
notes: Keri Ka Salad means raw mango salad in Hindi - a seasonal delicacy.

name: Onion Salad
preparation: Slice onions into thin rings and separate.
Arrange onion rings on a plate.
Sprinkle salt, red chilli powder, and lemon juice.
Add slit green chilli.
Let it sit for 5 minutes for flavors to meld.
Garnish with coriander. Serve as a side with any Indian meal.
notes: The simplest Indian salad, found at every dhaba and restaurant.

name: Pink Onion Salad
ingredients: 2 large pink onions, sliced into rings
preparation: Slice pink onions into thin rings.
Toss onion rings with a splash of beetroot juice for extra pink color.
Add lemon juice, salt, chaat masala, and sumac.
Mix gently.
Let rest for 10 minutes for the onions to soften slightly.
Serve as a vibrant side salad.
notes: Pink onions have a milder flavor and look beautiful on the plate.

name: Onion Chilli Salad
preparation: Slice onions into thin half-rings.
Slit green chillies lengthwise.
Combine onions and green chillies.
Add lemon juice, salt, and red chilli powder.
Mix well and let sit for 5 minutes.
Garnish with coriander. A fiery side for kebabs and tandoori.
notes: A spicy onion salad that's a must with tandoori dishes and kebabs.

name: Laccha Onion
preparation: Slice onions into very thin rings and separate all layers.
Soak rings in cold water for 10 minutes for crispness, then drain.
Toss with lemon juice, salt, Kashmiri red chilli powder, and chaat masala.
Spread on a plate.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve with tandoori chicken, kebabs, or biryani.
notes: Laccha means rings/layers. A classic accompaniment at North Indian restaurants.

name: Tomato Salad Indian
preparation: Slice tomatoes into round slices.
Slice onion into thin half-rings.
Arrange tomato and onion slices on a plate.
Sprinkle green chilli, salt, black pepper, and lemon juice.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve immediately as a refreshing side.
notes: Use firm, ripe tomatoes for the best texture in this simple salad.

name: Tomato Onion Green Chilli Salad
preparation: Dice tomatoes and onion into small uniform pieces.
Finely chop green chillies.
Combine all in a bowl.
Add lemon juice, salt, and cumin powder.
Toss well to mix.
Garnish with coriander. A spicy, tangy everyday salad.
notes: The holy trinity of Indian salads - tomato, onion, and green chilli.

name: Cherry Tomato Chaat
preparation: Halve cherry tomatoes.
Finely chop onion and green chilli.
Combine tomatoes, onion, and green chilli.
Sprinkle chaat masala, black salt, roasted cumin, and lemon juice.
Toss gently so tomatoes stay intact.
Garnish with coriander and serve as a chaat.
notes: Cherry tomatoes burst with sweetness, balanced by tangy chaat masala.

name: Cucumber Salad Indian
preparation: Peel and slice cucumber into thin rounds.
Slice onion into half-rings.
Arrange cucumber and onion on a plate.
Sprinkle green chilli, salt, chaat masala, cumin powder, and lemon juice.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve chilled for maximum freshness.
notes: A low-calorie, hydrating salad perfect for weight management.

name: Cucumber Mint Salad
preparation: Dice cucumber into small cubes.
Finely chop mint leaves and onion.
Combine cucumber, mint, and onion in a bowl.
Whisk lemon juice, salt, pepper, sugar, and olive oil.
Pour dressing over the salad and toss.
Serve chilled for a refreshing experience.
notes: Mint adds a cooling freshness to this light cucumber salad.

name: Cucumber Peanut Salad
preparation: Dice cucumber and crush roasted peanuts coarsely.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine cucumber, peanuts, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour the tempering over the salad.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Toss well and serve fresh.
notes: A crunchy South Indian salad with the protein boost of peanuts.

name: Moong Dal Salad
preparation: Soak whole moong dal for 2 hours, drain well.
Finely chop onion, tomato, cucumber, and green chilli.
Mix soaked dal with all chopped vegetables.
Add lemon juice, salt, and chaat masala.
Toss gently to combine.
Garnish with coriander and serve as a protein-rich salad.
notes: Soaked moong dal is crunchy and packed with plant protein.

name: Masoor Sprout Salad
preparation: Steam masoor sprouts for 5 minutes until just tender.
Drain and let cool.
Add chopped onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, and red chilli powder.
Toss well to combine.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Masoor sprouts cook quickly and are rich in iron and protein.

name: Mixed Bean Salad Indian
preparation: Boil all beans separately until tender, drain and cool.
Combine all beans in a large bowl.
Add chopped onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, chaat masala, and salt.
Toss gently to combine.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A protein-packed salad combining three different legumes.

name: Fruit Salad Indian
preparation: Wash and dice apple, papaya. Slice banana.
Combine all fruits in a bowl.
Sprinkle chaat masala, black salt, and red chilli powder.
Add lemon juice and toss gently.
Refrigerate for 10 minutes.
Serve chilled as a refreshing snack.
notes: Indian-style fruit salad with chaat masala gives a tangy twist.

name: Masala Fruit Salad
preparation: Cut all fruits into bite-sized pieces.
Combine all fruits in a large bowl.
Mix chaat masala, black salt, red chilli powder, lemon juice, and honey.
Pour the masala dressing over the fruits.
Toss gently to coat evenly.
Serve immediately or chill briefly.
notes: A zesty masala-dressed fruit bowl that's sweet, sour, and spicy.

name: Chaat Masala Fruit Bowl
preparation: Dice guava, pineapple, and apple into bite-sized pieces.
Combine all fruits with pomegranate arils.
Sprinkle chaat masala, black salt, and red chilli powder.
Drizzle lemon juice over the top.
Toss gently to coat.
Serve immediately for the freshest taste.
notes: Chaat masala transforms ordinary fruit into an addictive snack.

name: Dahi Fruit Salad
preparation: Whisk curd until smooth and creamy.
Dice apple, slice banana, and halve grapes.
Fold all fruits into the curd.
Drizzle honey and sprinkle cardamom powder.
Top with chopped nuts.
Serve immediately or chill for 10 minutes.
notes: A creamy fruit salad with curd dressing and a hint of cardamom.

name: Corn Salad Indian
preparation: Boil or steam corn kernels until tender.
Drain and let cool.
Add chopped onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, and red chilli powder.
Toss well to combine.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Sweet corn salad is a popular street food-style snack in India.

name: Corn Chaat Salad
preparation: Boil corn kernels and potato. Dice the potato.
Combine corn, potato, and onion in a bowl.
Drizzle tamarind and green chutney.
Add chaat masala and salt, toss well.
Top with sev for crunch.
Garnish with coriander and serve immediately.
notes: A chaat-style corn salad with sweet and tangy chutneys.

name: Sweet Corn Salad Indian
preparation: Cook corn kernels in butter for 2-3 minutes until lightly charred.
Let cool slightly.
Add diced bell pepper, cucumber, and onion.
Season with salt, black pepper, and lemon juice.
Toss to combine.
Garnish with coriander and serve warm or at room temperature.
notes: Butter-roasted corn adds a smoky sweetness to this simple salad.

name: Cabbage Salad Indian
preparation: Finely shred cabbage and grate carrot.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine cabbage, carrot, green chilli, and peanuts.
Pour tempering over the salad.
Add lemon juice, salt, and sugar.
Toss well and serve fresh.
notes: A crunchy Indian-style coleslaw with mustard seed tempering.

name: Cabbage Peanut Salad
preparation: Finely shred cabbage.
Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine cabbage, peanuts, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour the tempering over.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Toss well. Serve as a crunchy side dish.
notes: Peanuts and coconut add richness to this simple cabbage salad.

name: Cabbage Coconut Salad
preparation: Finely shred cabbage.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, and curry leaves.
When dal turns golden, pour tempering over cabbage.
Add grated coconut and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice and salt.
Mix well and serve at room temperature.
notes: A light South Indian salad with the tropical aroma of fresh coconut.

name: Radish Salad Indian
preparation: Peel and grate white radish.
Squeeze out excess water from the grated radish.
Add green chilli, lemon juice, salt, and red chilli powder.
Mix well to combine.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve immediately as a pungent, crunchy side.
notes: Fresh mooli salad aids digestion and is commonly served with parathas.

name: Mooli Salad
preparation: Peel and julienne mooli and carrot into thin strips.
Soak in ice water for 10 minutes for crispness, then drain.
Combine mooli and carrot strips.
Add green chilli, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and chaat masala.
Toss well.
Garnish with coriander and serve chilled.
notes: Julienned mooli is crisp and refreshing, great with rich North Indian meals.

name: Mooli Raita Salad
ingredients: 1 cup mooli (white radish), grated and squeezed
preparation: Grate mooli and squeeze out all excess water.
Whisk curd until smooth.
Add grated mooli to the curd.
Season with cumin powder, salt, green chilli, and red chilli powder.
Mix gently.
Garnish with coriander and serve chilled with parathas.
notes: A pungent raita-salad that pairs wonderfully with stuffed parathas.

name: Spinach Salad Indian
preparation: Wash baby spinach leaves thoroughly and pat dry.
Slice onion and dice tomato.
Combine spinach, onion, tomato, and roasted chana dal.
Add green chilli, lemon juice, salt, and chaat masala.
Toss gently so leaves don't bruise.
Garnish with coriander and serve fresh.
notes: Iron-rich spinach salad with crunchy roasted chana dal for protein.

name: Palak Raita Salad
preparation: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 1 minute, then dunk in ice water.
Squeeze out water and chop finely.
Whisk curd until smooth.
Add chopped spinach, cumin powder, salt, pepper, and green chilli.
Mix well until evenly combined.
Garnish with coriander and serve chilled.
notes: A nutritious green raita with the iron goodness of spinach.

name: Baby Spinach Chaat
preparation: Wash and dry baby spinach leaves.
Combine spinach, chickpeas, pomegranate, and onion.
Drizzle tamarind chutney and lemon juice.
Sprinkle chaat masala and black salt.
Toss gently.
Top with sev and serve immediately.
notes: A fusion chaat salad combining baby spinach with Indian flavors.

name: Mixed Veg Salad Indian
preparation: Dice cucumber, tomato, and bell pepper. Grate carrot. Shred cabbage.
Combine all vegetables in a large bowl.
Add lemon juice, salt, and chaat masala.
Toss well to mix evenly.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve as a wholesome side with any meal.
notes: A rainbow of vegetables for maximum nutrition in every bite.

name: Garden Salad Indian Style
preparation: Tear lettuce into bite-sized pieces.
Slice cucumber, cut tomato into wedges, julienne carrot.
Slice radish thinly and separate onion into rings.
Arrange all vegetables in a serving bowl.
Sprinkle lemon juice, salt, chaat masala, and cumin powder.
Toss lightly and serve as a fresh garden salad.
notes: A classic garden salad with Indian seasoning.

name: Seasonal Veg Salad
ingredients: 1/2 cup seasonal vegetables, diced (carrot, beans, peas)
preparation: Blanch seasonal vegetables (carrot, beans, peas) briefly, cool in ice water.
Dice cucumber and chop tomato and onion.
Combine all vegetables in a bowl.
Add green chilli, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
Toss well to combine.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Use whatever fresh seasonal vegetables are available for this adaptable salad.

name: Paneer Salad
preparation: Cut paneer into small cubes.
Dice cucumber, tomato, and bell pepper. Chop onion.
Combine paneer with all vegetables.
Season with lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, and black pepper.
Toss gently so paneer doesn't crumble.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Protein-rich paneer salad that's filling and satisfying.

name: Paneer Tikka Salad
preparation: Marinate paneer cubes in tikka masala paste and curd for 30 minutes.
Grill or pan-fry marinated paneer until charred edges appear.
Grill onion and bell pepper chunks alongside.
Arrange lettuce on a plate.
Top with grilled paneer, onion, bell pepper, and tomato wedges.
Sprinkle chaat masala, salt, and lemon juice. Serve warm.
notes: A hearty salad with smoky paneer tikka as the star ingredient.

name: Grilled Paneer Salad
preparation: Slice paneer into flat rectangles.
Brush with olive oil, season with salt, pepper, and oregano.
Grill on a hot pan until golden grill marks appear on both sides.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate.
Top with grilled paneer, cherry tomatoes, and cucumber.
Drizzle balsamic vinegar and lemon juice. Serve warm.
notes: A fusion salad combining grilled paneer with Mediterranean flavors.

name: Paneer Chaat Salad
preparation: Lightly pan-fry paneer cubes until golden on all sides.
Combine fried paneer with onion and tomato.
Drizzle tamarind chutney and green chutney.
Add chaat masala and salt, toss gently.
Top with sev, pomegranate arils, and coriander.
Serve immediately while paneer is still warm.
notes: A street food-inspired chaat salad with crispy paneer cubes.

name: Aloo Chaat Salad
preparation: Boil potatoes, peel, and cut into cubes.
Combine potato cubes with onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Drizzle tamarind and green chutneys generously.
Sprinkle chaat masala and salt.
Toss gently to coat potatoes.
Top with sev and coriander. Serve immediately.
notes: A popular street food chaat that doubles as a satisfying salad.

name: Potato Pea Salad Indian
preparation: Boil potatoes and peas separately. Cube the potatoes.
Combine potatoes, peas, onion, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, and cumin powder.
Toss gently to mix without mashing potatoes.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve at room temperature.
notes: A comforting potato-pea combination, seasoned the Indian way.

name: Chatpata Aloo Salad
ingredients: 1/2 cup raw mango, grated (or 2 tbsp amchur)
preparation: Boil potatoes, peel, and cube.
Grate raw mango (if using fresh).
Combine potato cubes, mango, onion, and green chilli.
Season with chaat masala, salt, red chilli powder, and black salt.
Mix gently.
Garnish with coriander. Serve as a tangy snack-salad.
notes: Chatpata means tangy-spicy. Raw mango adds the perfect sour kick.

name: Peanut Salad South Indian
preparation: Roast peanuts until golden and crunchy.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine peanuts, coconut, onion, and green chilli.
Pour the tempering over the mixture.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A high-protein South Indian peanut salad, great as a tea-time snack.

name: Roasted Peanut Salad
preparation: Use freshly roasted peanuts for best flavor.
Chop onion, tomato, green chilli, and coriander.
Combine peanuts with all vegetables.
Add lemon juice, salt, and chaat masala.
Toss well.
Serve immediately to maintain peanut crunch.
notes: Quick and easy protein-rich salad that comes together in minutes.

name: Peanut Cucumber Salad
preparation: Dice cucumber and chop onion.
Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine cucumber, peanuts, onion, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour tempering over the salad.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Toss and serve fresh.
notes: Cucumber adds freshness while peanuts provide satisfying crunch.

name: Bell Pepper Salad Indian
ingredients: 1 cup mixed bell peppers (red, yellow, green), diced
preparation: Dice bell peppers into uniform pieces.
Slice onion and dice tomato.
Combine all vegetables in a bowl.
Add green chilli, lemon juice, salt, chaat masala, and black pepper.
Toss well to coat evenly.
Garnish with coriander and serve colorful.
notes: Bell peppers are rich in Vitamin C and add crunch and color.

name: Capsicum Onion Salad
preparation: Slice capsicum into thin strips.
Slice onion into rings and tomato into rounds.
Arrange capsicum, onion, and tomato on a plate.
Add slit green chilli.
Sprinkle lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
Garnish with coriander. A simple, crunchy side.
notes: A straightforward salad that goes well with any Indian meal.

name: Tri Color Capsicum Salad
preparation: Dice all three bell peppers into uniform pieces.
Chop onion finely.
Combine peppers and onion.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, salt, oregano, and black pepper.
Pour dressing over the salad and toss.
Serve as a vibrant, crunchy side.
notes: A colorful salad that looks as good as it tastes.

name: Broccoli Salad Indian
preparation: Blanch broccoli florets in salted boiling water for 2 minutes.
Immediately transfer to ice water. Drain well.
Combine broccoli with onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Add peanuts, lemon juice, salt, and chaat masala.
Toss gently.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Blanching keeps broccoli bright green and perfectly tender-crisp.

name: Broccoli Peanut Salad
preparation: Blanch broccoli florets and cool in ice water. Drain.
Combine broccoli, peanuts, and grated carrot.
Add green chilli.
Whisk sesame oil, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
Pour dressing over salad and toss.
Sprinkle sesame seeds on top and serve.
notes: Sesame oil and peanuts give this broccoli salad an Asian twist.

name: Broccoli Corn Salad Indian
preparation: Blanch broccoli and boil corn kernels. Drain both.
Let cool to room temperature.
Combine broccoli, corn, onion, and bell pepper.
Add lemon juice, salt, chaat masala, and black pepper.
Toss well.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A nutritious combination of broccoli and sweet corn.

name: Sweet Potato Salad Indian
ingredients: 2 medium sweet potatoes, boiled and cubed
preparation: Boil sweet potatoes until tender, peel and cube.
Let cool to room temperature.
Add onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, and red chilli powder.
Toss gently to coat.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Sweet potatoes are rich in beta-carotene and make a filling salad.

name: Shakarkandi Chaat
ingredients: 2 medium sweet potatoes (shakarkandi), roasted and cubed
preparation: Roast sweet potatoes in oven at 200°C for 30-35 minutes or boil.
Peel and cut into cubes.
Season with chaat masala, black salt, red chilli powder, and cumin powder.
Add lemon juice and green chilli.
Toss well to coat.
Garnish with coriander. A popular winter street food.
notes: Shakarkandi chaat is a beloved winter snack in North India.

name: Roasted Sweet Potato Salad
preparation: Toss sweet potato cubes with olive oil, salt, pepper, and cumin.
Roast at 200°C for 25-30 minutes until golden.
Let cool slightly.
Combine with onion and pomegranate arils.
Drizzle with lemon juice.
Garnish with coriander and serve warm.
notes: Roasting caramelizes sweet potatoes and intensifies their natural sweetness.

name: Pomegranate Salad Indian
preparation: Extract pomegranate arils from the fruit.
Dice cucumber and chop onion.
Combine pomegranate, cucumber, onion, coconut, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice, salt, and chaat masala.
Toss gently to mix.
Garnish with coriander and serve chilled.
notes: Pomegranate arils are packed with antioxidants and add beautiful color.

name: Pomegranate Raita Bowl
preparation: Whisk curd until smooth.
Reserve a few pomegranate arils for garnish.
Fold remaining pomegranate arils into the curd.
Add cumin powder, salt, sugar, and black salt.
Mix gently.
Garnish with reserved arils and mint. Serve chilled.
notes: A jewel-like raita bowl that's as beautiful as it is delicious.

name: Pomegranate Mint Salad
preparation: Combine pomegranate arils, chopped mint, cucumber, and onion.
Add lemon juice, salt, black pepper, and chaat masala.
Toss gently to keep arils intact.
Chill for 5-10 minutes.
Serve as a refreshing appetizer or side.
Best consumed fresh for maximum crunch.
notes: Mint and pomegranate create a refreshing, antioxidant-rich combination.

name: Papaya Salad Indian
preparation: Peel ripe papaya, remove seeds, and cube.
Finely chop onion and green chilli.
Combine papaya, onion, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice, salt, chaat masala, and red chilli powder.
Toss gently so papaya doesn't mush.
Garnish with coriander and serve chilled.
notes: Ripe papaya is rich in enzymes that aid digestion.

name: Watermelon Salad Indian
preparation: Cut watermelon into bite-sized cubes, removing seeds.
Combine watermelon, onion, mint, and green chilli.
Sprinkle black salt, chaat masala, and cumin powder.
Add lemon juice and toss gently.
Chill for 10 minutes.
Serve cold on a hot summer day.
notes: An incredibly hydrating salad, perfect for Indian summers.

name: Muskmelon Salad Indian
preparation: Cut muskmelon into bite-sized cubes.
Dice cucumber.
Combine muskmelon, cucumber, and mint.
Sprinkle black salt, chaat masala, and black pepper.
Add lemon juice and toss gently.
Serve chilled for a refreshing treat.
notes: Muskmelon is naturally sweet and cooling, ideal for summer.

name: Sprouted Moth Bean Salad
preparation: Steam sprouted moth beans for 5-7 minutes.
Drain and cool.
Add onion, tomato, coconut, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, salt, and turmeric.
Toss to combine.
Garnish with coriander. A Maharashtrian favorite.
notes: Moth bean sprouts are a staple in Maharashtrian cuisine.

name: Rajma Salad Indian
preparation: Boil rajma until soft or use canned, drained.
Let cool to room temperature.
Add onion, tomato, cucumber, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, chaat masala, and salt.
Toss well.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Rajma in salad form is a protein-packed alternative to the curry version.

name: Lobhia Salad
preparation: Boil lobhia until tender, drain and cool.
Add chopped onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, and cumin powder.
Mix well.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve as a protein-rich salad.
notes: Black-eyed peas are easy to digest and great in cold salads.

name: Avocado Salad Indian
preparation: Cut avocado in half, remove pit, and cube the flesh.
Combine avocado with onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice immediately to prevent browning.
Season with salt, chaat masala, and cumin powder.
Toss very gently to keep avocado pieces intact.
Garnish with coriander and serve immediately.
notes: Avocado's creamy texture pairs beautifully with Indian spices.

name: Avocado Chaat
preparation: Cube avocado and place in a bowl.
Add chickpeas, onion, and tomato.
Sprinkle chaat masala and black salt.
Drizzle lemon juice and tamarind chutney.
Toss gently.
Top with sev and coriander. Serve immediately.
notes: A trendy fusion chaat that combines creamy avocado with tangy masalas.

name: Avocado Corn Salad Indian
preparation: Cube avocado and boil corn kernels.
Combine avocado, corn, onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice to prevent avocado browning.
Season with salt and chaat masala.
Toss gently.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A creamy and sweet combination of avocado and corn.

name: Mushroom Salad Indian
preparation: Clean and slice mushrooms. Sauté in olive oil until golden.
Season with salt, pepper, and cumin while sautéing.
Let mushrooms cool.
Combine with onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice and toss.
Garnish with coriander and serve warm or at room temperature.
notes: Sautéed mushrooms have a meaty texture perfect for salads.

name: Button Mushroom Chaat
preparation: Quarter the mushrooms.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds, then sauté mushrooms for 3-4 minutes.
Let cool slightly.
Add onion, tomato, chaat masala, and red chilli powder.
Drizzle lemon juice and toss.
Top with sev and coriander. Serve as a chaat.
notes: A unique chaat using mushrooms instead of traditional potatoes.

name: Grilled Mushroom Salad
preparation: Toss mushrooms with olive oil, salt, pepper, and herbs.
Grill on a hot pan or grill for 3-4 minutes per side.
Let cool slightly and slice.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate.
Top with grilled mushrooms and cherry tomatoes.
Drizzle balsamic vinegar and lemon juice. Serve warm.
notes: Grilling intensifies mushroom flavor and adds a smoky touch.

name: Egg Salad Indian
preparation: Hard-boil eggs for 10 minutes, peel and chop.
Finely chop onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Gently combine eggs with vegetables.
Season with lemon juice, salt, pepper, and chaat masala.
Toss very gently to keep egg pieces intact.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A protein-rich egg salad with Indian spices, no mayo needed.

name: Boiled Egg Chaat Salad
preparation: Hard-boil eggs, peel and cut into quarters.
Arrange eggs on a plate.
Top with onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Drizzle tamarind chutney.
Sprinkle chaat masala, salt, and red chilli powder.
Garnish with coriander. A protein-packed chaat.
notes: A street food-style egg chaat, popular at roadside stalls.

name: Egg Onion Salad
preparation: Hard-boil eggs and slice into rounds.
Slice onion into thin rings.
Arrange alternating egg slices and onion rings on a plate.
Add slit green chilli.
Sprinkle lemon juice, salt, black pepper, and red chilli powder.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A classic combination served at dhabas across India.

name: Chicken Tikka Salad Indian
preparation: Marinate chicken cubes in tikka paste and curd for 1 hour.
Grill or pan-fry chicken until cooked through with charred edges.
Let cool slightly.
Arrange lettuce on a plate.
Top with chicken tikka, onion, tomato, and cucumber.
Sprinkle chaat masala, drizzle lemon juice, and garnish with coriander.
notes: A high-protein salad featuring smoky chicken tikka pieces.

name: Tandoori Chicken Salad
preparation: Marinate chicken in tandoori marinade and curd for 2 hours.
Grill or bake at 200°C for 20 minutes until charred and cooked through.
Slice chicken into strips.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate.
Top with tandoori chicken strips, onion, and cucumber.
Drizzle mint chutney and lemon juice. Garnish with coriander.
notes: A restaurant-style salad with smoky tandoori chicken.

name: Grilled Chicken Salad Indian
preparation: Season chicken with olive oil, salt, pepper, and garam masala.
Grill for 6-7 minutes per side until fully cooked.
Let rest for 5 minutes, then slice.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate.
Top with sliced chicken, cherry tomatoes, cucumber, and onion.
Drizzle lemon juice and garnish with coriander.
notes: A lean, protein-packed salad ideal for fitness-focused diets.

name: Fish Salad Indian
preparation: Season fish with turmeric, red chilli powder, and salt.
Pan-fry in oil until golden and cooked through. Flake into pieces.
Let cool.
Combine fish flakes with onion, cucumber, and tomato.
Add lemon juice and toss gently.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A light fish salad with Indian spices, great for seafood lovers.

name: Grilled Fish Salad Indian
preparation: Marinate fish with lemon juice, salt, pepper, cumin, and oil.
Grill fish for 4-5 minutes per side until cooked.
Let cool slightly and flake or slice.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate.
Top with grilled fish, onion, tomato, and cucumber.
Drizzle green chutney and serve.
notes: Grilled fish is light and pairs beautifully with fresh greens.

name: Prawn Salad Indian
preparation: Clean and devein prawns. Season with turmeric, chilli powder, and salt.
Sauté prawns in oil for 3-4 minutes until pink and cooked.
Let cool.
Combine prawns with onion, cucumber, and tomato.
Add lemon juice and black pepper.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Prawns cook quickly and make an excellent salad protein.

name: Bean Salad Indian
ingredients: 1 cup French beans, cut into 1-inch pieces and blanched
preparation: Cut French beans and blanch in boiling water for 3 minutes.
Drain and cool in ice water.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine beans, onion, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour tempering over the salad.
Add lemon juice and salt. Toss and serve.
notes: A simple South Indian-style green bean salad with coconut.

name: French Bean Salad
ingredients: 1.5 cups French beans, diagonally sliced and blanched
preparation: Blanch French beans for 2-3 minutes, cool in ice water.
Julienne carrot and slice onion.
Combine beans, carrot, onion, and peanuts.
Whisk lemon juice, salt, pepper, and sesame oil.
Pour dressing over the salad.
Toss well and serve.
notes: Diagonal-cut beans look elegant and cook more evenly.

name: Cluster Bean Salad
ingredients: 1.5 cups cluster beans (guar), chopped and blanched
preparation: Chop cluster beans and blanch for 3-4 minutes.
Drain and cool.
Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine beans, onion, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour tempering over.
Add lemon juice and salt. Mix well and serve.
notes: Cluster beans (guar phali) are rich in fiber and aid digestion.

name: Tofu Salad Indian
preparation: Cube tofu and pat dry.
Season with turmeric, red chilli powder, and salt.
Pan-fry in oil until golden and crispy on all sides.
Let cool slightly.
Combine tofu with onion, tomato, and cucumber.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala, and coriander. Toss gently.
notes: Pan-fried tofu adds a protein-rich, crispy element to salads.

name: Soya Chunk Salad
ingredients: 1 cup soya chunks, boiled and squeezed
preparation: Boil soya chunks in salted water for 5 minutes. Drain and squeeze out water.
Let cool and cut larger pieces in half.
Combine soya chunks with onion, tomato, cucumber, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, and red chilli powder.
Toss well.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Soya chunks are an affordable, high-protein vegetarian ingredient.

name: Quinoa Salad Indian
preparation: Cook quinoa as per package instructions. Fluff with fork and cool.
Dice cucumber, tomato, bell pepper, and chop onion.
Combine quinoa with all vegetables.
Add green chilli, lemon juice, salt, and cumin powder.
Toss well to mix.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Quinoa is a complete protein grain, perfect for healthy salads.

name: Millet Salad Indian
preparation: Cook foxtail millet, fluff and let cool.
Dice cucumber and bell pepper, grate carrot.
Combine millet with all vegetables.
Add green chilli, lemon juice, salt, and cumin powder.
Toss well.
Garnish with coriander and serve at room temperature.
notes: Millets are ancient grains making a healthy comeback in modern diets.

name: Jowar Salad
preparation: Soak jowar overnight and pressure cook until tender. Drain and cool.
Dice vegetables uniformly.
Combine cooked jowar with all vegetables.
Season with lemon juice, salt, and chaat masala.
Toss to mix.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Jowar is gluten-free and rich in fiber, making it ideal for healthy eating.

name: Bajra Salad
preparation: Cook bajra grains until tender, drain and cool.
Dice cucumber, chop onion, grate carrot.
Combine bajra with vegetables and boiled peas.
Add green chilli, lemon juice, salt, and cumin powder.
Mix well.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Bajra is a winter superfood, high in iron and excellent for health.

name: Lettuce Chaat
preparation: Tear lettuce into bite-sized pieces.
Combine lettuce, chickpeas, onion, and tomato.
Drizzle tamarind chutney over the salad.
Sprinkle chaat masala and black salt.
Toss gently.
Top with sev, pomegranate arils, and coriander.
notes: A fusion chaat that makes lettuce exciting with Indian toppings.

name: Iceberg Lettuce Salad Indian
preparation: Shred iceberg lettuce into bite-sized pieces.
Dice cucumber and tomato, chop onion, grate carrot.
Combine all vegetables with lettuce.
Season with lemon juice, salt, chaat masala, and black pepper.
Toss well.
Serve chilled for maximum crunch.
notes: Iceberg lettuce is crisp and hydrating, low in calories.

name: Romaine Indian Salad
preparation: Chop romaine lettuce into 1-inch pieces.
Combine with chickpeas, onion, tomato, and cucumber.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and cumin powder.
Pour dressing over salad.
Toss gently to coat.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Romaine lettuce has more nutrients than iceberg and a pleasant crunch.

name: Baby Corn Salad Indian
ingredients: 1.5 cups baby corn, sliced and blanched
preparation: Slice baby corn diagonally and blanch for 2 minutes. Cool.
Dice bell pepper and tomato, chop onion.
Combine baby corn with all vegetables.
Add lemon juice, salt, and chaat masala.
Toss well.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Baby corn has a mild, sweet flavor and satisfying crunch.

name: Corn Capsicum Salad
preparation: Boil corn kernels and let cool.
Dice bell peppers uniformly.
Combine corn, bell peppers, onion, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice, salt, black pepper, and oregano.
Toss well.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A colorful and popular combination loved by kids and adults alike.

name: Grilled Corn Salad Indian
ingredients: 2 corn cobs, grilled and kernels removed
preparation: Grill corn cobs over open flame or on a grill pan until charred.
Remove kernels from the cob.
Toss warm kernels with butter.
Add onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Season with salt, chaat masala, red chilli powder, and lemon juice.
Garnish with coriander. Serve warm.
notes: Grilled corn has a smoky flavor reminiscent of Indian street bhutta.

name: Curd Salad Indian
preparation: Whisk curd until smooth.
Dice cucumber and tomato, chop onion, grate carrot.
Fold all vegetables into the curd.
Add cumin powder, salt, and green chilli.
Mix gently.
Garnish with coriander and serve chilled.
notes: A versatile curd-based salad that doubles as a thick raita.

name: Dahi Vegetable Salad
preparation: Whisk curd until smooth and creamy.
Dice all vegetables finely.
Fold vegetables and pomegranate into the curd.
Season with cumin powder, salt, and chaat masala.
Mix gently.
Garnish with coriander. Serve chilled as a refreshing side.
notes: A loaded curd salad with vegetables and pomegranate for extra nutrition.

name: Hung Curd Salad
preparation: Hang curd in muslin cloth for 2 hours to drain whey.
Finely dice bell pepper, cucumber, and onion.
Mix vegetables into the hung curd.
Add salt, pepper, oregano, olive oil, and lemon juice.
Stir gently to combine.
Garnish with fresh mint and serve chilled.
notes: Hung curd has a thick, creamy texture similar to Greek yogurt.

name: Tamarind Salad Indian
preparation: Combine chickpeas, potato, onion, and tomato.
Drizzle tamarind chutney and green chutney generously.
Sprinkle chaat masala and salt.
Toss to coat everything with the tangy chutney.
Top with sev for crunch.
Garnish with coriander and serve immediately.
notes: Tamarind gives this chaat salad its signature sweet-sour flavor.

name: Imli Aloo Salad
preparation: Boil potatoes, peel, and cube.
Place potato cubes in a bowl.
Drizzle tamarind chutney generously over potatoes.
Add onion, green chilli, chaat masala, salt, and red chilli powder.
Toss gently.
Top with sev and coriander. Serve as a tangy snack.
notes: Imli (tamarind) and aloo (potato) is a beloved street food combination.

name: Tangy Chaat Salad
preparation: Combine chickpeas, potato, sweet potato, onion, and raw mango.
Drizzle both chutneys over the mixture.
Add chaat masala and black salt.
Toss well to coat.
Top with sev and coriander.
Serve immediately for the best texture.
notes: A medley of textures and flavors in every bite of this tangy chaat.

name: Mixed Raita Bowl
preparation: Whisk curd until smooth.
Soak boondi in warm water for 5 minutes, drain.
Add cucumber, onion, tomato, and boondi to curd.
Season with cumin powder, salt, and chaat masala.
Mix gently.
Garnish with coriander. Serve chilled.
notes: A loaded raita bowl combining vegetables and boondi.

name: Vegetable Raita Salad
preparation: Whisk curd until smooth.
Finely dice cucumber and bell pepper, grate carrot, chop onion.
Fold all vegetables into the curd.
Add cumin powder, salt, and black pepper.
Mix gently.
Garnish with coriander and serve chilled.
notes: A nutritious raita loaded with colorful vegetables.

name: Fruit Raita Bowl
preparation: Whisk curd until smooth and creamy.
Dice apple, slice banana, and halve grapes.
Fold all fruits into the curd.
Drizzle honey and sprinkle cardamom powder.
Add raisins.
Serve chilled as a sweet raita-salad.
notes: A sweet fruit raita that kids and adults both love.

name: Sundal Salad
preparation: Boil chana dal until just tender, not mushy. Drain.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, red chilli, curry leaves, and asafoetida.
Add boiled chana dal and toss with the tempering.
Add salt and mix well.
Garnish with grated coconut and green chilli.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
notes: Sundal is a traditional South Indian temple and festival offering.

name: Black Eyed Pea Sundal
preparation: Boil black-eyed peas until tender. Drain.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, red chilli, curry leaves, and asafoetida.
Add boiled peas and mix with the tempering.
Season with salt.
Top with grated coconut and green chilli.
Serve warm as a protein-rich snack-salad.
notes: A popular Navaratri sundal variety using black-eyed peas.

name: Peanut Sundal Salad
ingredients: 1 cup boiled peanuts (or soaked raw peanuts)
preparation: Boil peanuts until tender or soak overnight and cook.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, red chilli, curry leaves, and asafoetida.
Add cooked peanuts and toss with tempering.
Season with salt.
Garnish with grated coconut and green chilli.
Serve warm. A beloved temple sundal.
notes: Peanut sundal is the most popular sundal variety in South India.

name: Coconut Chutney Salad Bowl
preparation: Blend coconut, chana dal, and green chilli into a coarse chutney.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine chutney with cucumber, onion, and tomato.
Pour tempering over the mixture.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Serve as a unique chutney-salad bowl.
notes: A creative salad bowl inspired by South Indian coconut chutney.

name: South Indian Salad Plate
preparation: Prepare all vegetables: slice, julienne, grate, and shred.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Arrange vegetables on a plate in separate sections.
Pour tempering over.
Sprinkle coconut, lemon juice, and salt.
Serve as a colorful South Indian salad thali.
notes: A traditional South Indian salad plate served with rice meals.

name: Gujarati Kachumber
preparation: Dice all vegetables into small uniform pieces.
Combine cucumber, tomato, onion, and raw mango in a bowl.
Add green chilli, lemon juice, salt, sugar, and cumin powder.
Toss well.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve as a side with Gujarati thali.
notes: The Gujarati version adds a touch of sugar for sweet-sour balance.

name: Maharashtrian Koshimbir
preparation: Grate cucumber and squeeze out excess water.
Whisk curd and mix in grated cucumber.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Pour tempering over the curd-cucumber mixture.
Add peanuts, green chilli, salt, and sugar.
Garnish with coriander. A classic Maharashtrian side.
notes: Koshimbir is the Marathi word for raita-style salad with peanuts.

name: Raw Banana Salad
preparation: Boil raw bananas until tender, peel and dice.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine diced banana with onion, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour tempering over the mixture.
Add lemon juice, salt, and turmeric.
Mix gently and serve.
notes: Raw banana has a starchy, mild flavor similar to potato.

name: Plantain Salad Indian
preparation: Boil plantains until tender, peel and slice into rounds.
Combine with onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice, salt, and red chilli powder.
Toss gently so plantain slices stay intact.
Top with grated coconut.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Boiled plantain is a staple in Kerala and South Indian cuisines.

name: Green Banana Salad
preparation: Boil green bananas in their skin until tender.
Peel and cut into cubes.
Combine with onion and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, salt, chaat masala, and cumin powder.
Toss gently.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Green bananas are rich in resistant starch, beneficial for gut health.

name: Ridge Gourd Salad
ingredients: 1.5 cups ridge gourd (turai), peeled and grated
preparation: Peel ridge gourd and grate finely.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine grated ridge gourd, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour tempering over.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Mix well and serve fresh. A light, cooling salad.
notes: Ridge gourd is very low in calories and rich in dietary fiber.

name: Bitter Gourd Salad Light
preparation: Slice bitter gourd thinly and rub with salt. Let sit 15 minutes.
Squeeze out bitter juices and rinse.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds, then sauté bitter gourd slices for 3-4 minutes.
Let cool.
Add onion, lemon juice, red chilli powder, and turmeric.
Toss and serve. An acquired taste with great health benefits.
notes: Bitter gourd helps regulate blood sugar and is excellent for diabetics.

name: Snake Gourd Salad
preparation: Peel snake gourd and grate.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, and curry leaves.
Combine grated snake gourd, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour tempering over.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Mix and serve as a light side dish.
notes: Snake gourd is a mild, cooling vegetable popular in South India.

name: Drumstick Salad
preparation: Boil drumstick pieces until tender.
Scoop out the soft pulp from inside the fibrous shell.
Combine drumstick pulp with onion, tomato, and coconut.
Add green chilli, lemon juice, and salt.
Mix gently.
Serve as a nutritious side dish rich in iron and calcium.
notes: Drumstick (moringa) is a nutritional powerhouse loaded with vitamins.

name: Ash Gourd Salad
preparation: Peel ash gourd, remove seeds, and grate.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine grated ash gourd, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour tempering over.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Toss and serve as a cooling salad.
notes: Ash gourd has cooling properties and is used in Ayurvedic remedies.

name: Raw Jackfruit Salad
ingredients: 1 cup raw jackfruit, boiled and shredded
preparation: Boil raw jackfruit pieces until tender. Drain and shred.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine shredded jackfruit, onion, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour tempering over.
Add lemon juice, salt, and turmeric.
Mix well and serve.
notes: Raw jackfruit has a meaty texture and is a popular meat substitute.

name: Mint Coriander Salad
ingredients: 1 cup fresh mint leaves, roughly chopped
preparation: Wash and roughly chop mint and coriander.
Finely chop onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Combine herbs with vegetables.
Add lemon juice, salt, and chaat masala.
Toss well.
Serve as a fragrant, herb-forward salad.
notes: An herb-heavy salad that doubles as a natural mouth freshener.

name: Green Chutney Salad Bowl
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (cucumber, tomato, onion, carrot), diced
preparation: Blend mint, coriander, green chilli, lemon juice, and salt into a chutney.
Dice all vegetables and combine with chickpeas.
Pour green chutney over the vegetables.
Add roasted peanuts.
Toss well to coat everything with the chutney.
Serve as a vibrant green salad bowl.
notes: Green chutney as a dressing gives this salad an intensely herbal flavor.

name: Herb Salad Indian
preparation: Wash and chop all herbs.
Dice cucumber and chop spring onion.
Combine all herbs and vegetables.
Add green chilli, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
Toss gently.
Serve as a fragrant, nutrient-dense salad.
notes: Herbs are packed with antioxidants and make a refreshing salad base.

name: Sesame Salad Indian
preparation: Toast sesame seeds in a dry pan until golden.
Dice cucumber, grate carrot, chop onion.
Combine vegetables in a bowl.
Whisk sesame oil, lemon juice, salt, and sugar.
Pour dressing over vegetables and toss.
Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds on top and serve.
notes: Sesame seeds are rich in calcium and add a nutty flavor.

name: Til Chaat Salad
preparation: Toast sesame seeds until golden and aromatic.
Combine sesame seeds with grated jaggery and peanuts.
Add cucumber and onion.
Season with lemon juice and salt.
Mix well.
Garnish with coriander. A winter special salad.
notes: Til and jaggery are a traditional Makar Sankranti combination.

name: Flax Seed Salad Indian
ingredients: 2 tbsp flax seeds, roasted and coarsely ground
preparation: Roast flax seeds and coarsely grind them.
Dice cucumber and tomato, chop onion.
Combine all vegetables.
Add flax seed powder, lemon juice, and salt.
Toss well to coat.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Flax seeds are rich in omega-3 fatty acids and fiber.

name: Nachni Salad
preparation: Cook ragi grains until tender (or use ragi flakes). Cool.
Dice cucumber and tomato, chop onion.
Combine ragi with all vegetables.
Add green chilli, lemon juice, salt, and cumin powder.
Mix well.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Ragi/Nachni is rich in calcium and iron, a superfood millet.

name: Ragi Salad Bowl
preparation: Cook ragi grains and let cool completely.
Dice cucumber, bell pepper, and chop onion. Grate carrot.
Combine ragi with all vegetables.
Whisk lemon juice, salt, pepper, and olive oil.
Pour dressing over salad and toss.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A modern grain bowl featuring the ancient superfood ragi.

name: Multigrain Salad Indian
preparation: Cook all grains separately until tender. Cool.
Combine all grains in a large bowl.
Add diced cucumber, tomato, onion, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, salt, and cumin powder.
Toss well to combine.
Garnish with coriander. A nutritional powerhouse.
notes: Three ancient grains combine for maximum nutrition in one bowl.

name: Amla Salad
preparation: Grate amla, discarding the seed.
Add salt and let sit for 10 minutes to reduce tartness.
Add carrot, red chilli powder, sugar, and lemon juice.
Mix well.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve in small portions as amla is very tangy.
notes: Amla is the richest natural source of Vitamin C.

name: Gooseberry Chaat Salad
preparation: Grate amla, removing the seeds.
Mix with chaat masala, black salt, and red chilli powder.
Drizzle honey and lemon juice.
Add pomegranate arils.
Toss gently.
Serve as a tangy, immune-boosting snack-salad.
notes: Amla with chaat masala is a popular winter immunity booster.

name: Star Fruit Salad Indian
preparation: Slice star fruit into thin star-shaped slices.
Remove any seeds.
Arrange on a plate with onion and green chilli.
Sprinkle salt, chaat masala, and red chilli powder.
Drizzle lemon juice.
Garnish with coriander. A visually stunning salad.
notes: Star fruit is tangy-sweet and makes beautiful star-shaped slices.

name: Jackfruit Salad Indian
preparation: Remove seeds from ripe jackfruit and dice the flesh.
Slice banana.
Combine jackfruit, banana, and coconut.
Drizzle honey and lime juice.
Sprinkle cardamom powder.
Toss gently and serve as a sweet tropical salad.
notes: Ripe jackfruit is naturally sweet and fragrant.

name: Raw Papaya Salad
preparation: Peel raw papaya, remove seeds, and julienne.
Crush peanuts coarsely.
Combine papaya, peanuts, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice, salt, sugar, and red chilli powder.
Toss well.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Raw papaya is rich in digestive enzymes and fiber.

name: Tapioca Salad Indian
preparation: Soak sabudana for 4-5 hours, then steam until translucent.
Let cool.
Add peanuts, onion, and green chilli.
Season with lemon juice, salt, and cumin powder.
Toss gently so sabudana doesn't break.
Garnish with coriander. Great during fasting days.
notes: A fasting-friendly salad popular during Navratri in India.

name: Ivy Gourd Salad
preparation: Wash and thinly slice ivy gourd.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine sliced ivy gourd, onion, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour tempering over.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Toss and serve as a crunchy raw salad.
notes: Ivy gourd helps regulate blood sugar and is very low calorie.

name: Tindora Salad
preparation: Wash and slice tindora into thin rounds.
Combine with onion slices and slit green chilli.
Add lemon juice, salt, red chilli powder, and turmeric.
Toss well.
Let sit for 5 minutes for flavors to meld.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Tindora is a humble Indian vegetable that makes a great raw salad.

name: Cluster Bean Salad Indian
preparation: Chop cluster beans into small pieces and blanch for 3 minutes.
Drain and cool.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine beans, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour tempering over. Add salt and turmeric.
Mix well and serve.
notes: Cluster beans are high in fiber and help lower cholesterol.

name: Tendli Salad
preparation: Thinly slice tendli.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Combine tendli, peanuts, coconut, and green chilli.
Pour tempering over.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Toss and serve as a Maharashtrian-style salad.
notes: Tendli with peanuts and coconut is a Maharashtrian classic.

name: Raw Turmeric Salad
ingredients: 2 tbsp fresh raw turmeric, peeled and grated
preparation: Peel and finely grate fresh turmeric root.
Dice cucumber and chop onion.
Combine all ingredients in a bowl.
Add lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
Toss well. The turmeric will color everything golden.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Raw turmeric is a powerful anti-inflammatory. Use gloves while grating.

name: Ginger Salad Indian
preparation: Peel and julienne fresh ginger very finely.
Soak ginger strips in salted water for 5 minutes to mellow the heat.
Drain and combine with cucumber and onion.
Add lemon juice, salt, sugar, and red chilli powder.
Toss well.
Garnish with coriander. Serve in small portions.
notes: Ginger aids digestion and adds a warm, spicy kick.

name: Water Chestnut Salad
ingredients: 1 cup water chestnuts (singhara), boiled and sliced
preparation: Boil water chestnuts, peel and slice.
Combine with onion, cucumber, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice, salt, chaat masala, and black pepper.
Toss gently.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve as a crunchy salad, popular during Navratri fasting.
notes: Water chestnuts are a fasting food with a unique crunchy texture.

name: Caesar Salad Light
preparation: Chop romaine lettuce into bite-sized pieces.
Make dressing: whisk olive oil, lemon juice, mustard, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Toss lettuce with the dressing.
Add croutons and shaved Parmesan.
Toss gently once more.
Serve immediately for crisp lettuce.
notes: A lighter version of Caesar salad without heavy mayo-based dressing.

name: Classic Greek Salad
preparation: Cut cucumber and tomato into large chunks.
Slice red onion thinly.
Combine cucumber, tomato, onion, and olives.
Crumble feta cheese on top.
Whisk olive oil, red wine vinegar, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing over and serve without tossing too much.
notes: An authentic Greek salad with chunky vegetables and creamy feta.

name: Caprese Salad
preparation: Slice tomatoes and mozzarella into equal-thickness rounds.
Arrange alternating slices of tomato and mozzarella on a plate.
Tuck fresh basil leaves between the slices.
Drizzle olive oil and balsamic vinegar.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Serve immediately at room temperature.
notes: A classic Italian salad that showcases the finest fresh ingredients.

name: Nicoise Salad Light
preparation: Arrange lettuce on a plate.
Place tuna in the center.
Arrange eggs, green beans, tomatoes, olives, and potatoes around.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing over the composed salad.
Serve as an elegant, protein-rich meal.
notes: A French Riviera classic with tuna and eggs. A complete meal.

name: Cobb Salad Light
ingredients: 2 slices turkey bacon, cooked and crumbled
preparation: Chop romaine lettuce and place in a large bowl.
Arrange grilled chicken, eggs, bacon, tomatoes, avocado, and cheese in rows.
Whisk olive oil, red wine vinegar, and salt.
Drizzle dressing over the salad.
Toss just before eating.
A hearty, protein-packed American classic.
notes: A lighter Cobb salad with reduced dressing and turkey bacon.

name: Waldorf Salad Light
preparation: Dice apple (with skin) and toss with lemon juice to prevent browning.
Slice celery and halve grapes.
Combine apple, celery, grapes, and walnuts.
Mix Greek yogurt with salt for the dressing.
Toss salad with the yogurt dressing.
Serve on a bed of lettuce.
notes: A lighter Waldorf using Greek yogurt instead of heavy mayo.

name: Asian Sesame Salad
preparation: Combine mixed greens, cabbage, carrot, and edamame.
Make dressing: whisk sesame oil, soy sauce, rice vinegar, honey, and ginger.
Pour dressing over the salad.
Toss well to coat.
Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds on top.
Serve immediately.
notes: A light Asian-inspired salad with a nutty sesame dressing.

name: Quinoa Tabbouleh
preparation: Cook quinoa and let cool completely.
Finely chop parsley, mint, tomato, and cucumber.
Combine quinoa with all herbs and vegetables.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing and toss well.
Refrigerate for 30 minutes for flavors to meld.
notes: A gluten-free twist on traditional Lebanese tabbouleh using quinoa.

name: Mediterranean Quinoa Salad
preparation: Cook quinoa, fluff, and cool.
Halve cherry tomatoes and olives, dice cucumber and onion.
Combine quinoa with all vegetables and olives.
Add crumbled feta.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, and salt.
Drizzle dressing, toss, and serve.
notes: Mediterranean flavors meet the superfood quinoa.

name: Lemon Herb Quinoa Salad
preparation: Cook quinoa and cool completely.
Chop herbs and dice vegetables.
Combine quinoa with herbs and vegetables.
Whisk lemon juice, olive oil, lemon zest, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing over salad and toss.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.
notes: Fresh lemon and herbs make this quinoa salad bright and zesty.

name: Fattoush Salad
ingredients: 1 small pita bread, toasted and broken
preparation: Chop lettuce, dice tomato and cucumber, slice radish and onion.
Toast pita bread until crispy, then break into pieces.
Combine all vegetables and mint.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, sumac, and salt.
Pour dressing over salad and toss.
Add pita chips on top and serve immediately.
notes: A Lebanese bread salad with tangy sumac dressing.

name: Israeli Couscous Salad
preparation: Cook couscous according to package directions. Drain and cool.
Halve cherry tomatoes, dice cucumber and bell pepper.
Combine couscous with all vegetables and parsley.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and cumin.
Pour dressing and toss.
Serve at room temperature or chilled.
notes: Israeli couscous has a satisfying chewy texture unlike regular couscous.

name: Moroccan Carrot Salad
preparation: Grate or julienne carrots.
Soak raisins in warm water for 10 minutes, drain.
Combine carrots, raisins, and almonds.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, orange juice, cumin, cinnamon, and salt.
Pour dressing over carrots and toss.
Garnish with parsley and serve.
notes: A fragrant Moroccan-spiced carrot salad with warm spice notes.

name: Thai Mango Salad
ingredients: 1 tbsp fish sauce (or soy sauce for veg)
preparation: Julienne green mango into thin strips.
Slice red onion and red chilli.
Combine mango, onion, chilli, and crushed peanuts.
Whisk fish sauce (or soy sauce), lime juice, sugar, and sesame oil.
Pour dressing and toss well.
Garnish with cilantro and serve.
notes: A vibrant Thai salad with the perfect sweet-sour-salty-spicy balance.

name: Vietnamese Noodle Salad
preparation: Cook rice vermicelli, drain and cool under running water.
Julienne carrot and cucumber.
Make dressing: whisk soy sauce, lime juice, sugar, and sesame oil.
Combine noodles, vegetables, mint, and cilantro.
Pour dressing and toss well.
Top with peanuts and chilli slices.
notes: A refreshing Vietnamese-style cold noodle salad.

name: Korean Cucumber Salad
preparation: Thinly slice cucumber and sprinkle with salt. Let sit 10 minutes.
Squeeze out excess water from cucumber.
Whisk gochugaru, sesame oil, rice vinegar, soy sauce, sugar, and garlic.
Toss cucumber with the dressing.
Sprinkle sesame seeds and green onion.
Serve chilled as a banchan (side dish).
notes: Oi Muchim - a classic Korean cucumber side dish.

name: Spinach Strawberry Salad
preparation: Wash and dry baby spinach.
Slice strawberries.
Combine spinach, strawberries, walnuts, and goat cheese.
Whisk olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing over the salad.
Toss gently and serve immediately.
notes: A beautiful spring salad with sweet strawberries and tangy goat cheese.

name: Arugula Pear Salad
preparation: Wash arugula and place in a bowl.
Slice pear thinly.
Add pear slices and toasted walnuts.
Shave Parmesan over the top.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, honey, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing and toss gently.
notes: Peppery arugula pairs beautifully with sweet pear.

name: Baby Kale Salad
preparation: Wash baby kale and place in a bowl.
Add dried cranberries and pumpkin seeds.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, mustard, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing over kale and massage for 1 minute to soften.
Add shaved Parmesan.
Toss and serve.
notes: Massaging kale with dressing breaks down the fibers and reduces bitterness.

name: Southwest Black Bean Salad
preparation: Combine black beans, corn, tomato, onion, and bell pepper.
Whisk olive oil, lime juice, cumin, salt, and chilli powder.
Pour dressing over the salad.
Add cilantro and toss well.
Refrigerate for 15 minutes.
Serve chilled as a filling side.
notes: A hearty Tex-Mex salad packed with fiber and protein.

name: Mexican Street Corn Salad
ingredients: 1/4 cup cotija cheese or feta, crumbled
preparation: Grill corn kernels in a hot pan until slightly charred.
Transfer to a bowl.
Mix with light mayonnaise and lime juice.
Add crumbled cheese, chilli powder, and salt.
Toss well.
Garnish with cilantro. Inspired by Mexican elote.
notes: Esquites - Mexican street corn salad in a cup.

name: Tex Mex Salad
preparation: Chop romaine lettuce and place in a bowl.
Add black beans, corn, tomato, avocado, and onion.
Combine salsa, lime juice, cumin, and salt for dressing.
Pour dressing over salad and toss.
Top with crushed tortilla chips.
Serve immediately for crunch.
notes: A fun Tex-Mex bowl salad with all the classic flavors.

name: Tuna Salad Mediterranean
preparation: Drain tuna and flake into chunks.
Halve cherry tomatoes and olives, dice cucumber, slice onion.
Combine tuna with vegetables, olives, and capers.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and oregano.
Pour dressing and toss gently.
Serve over a bed of mixed greens.
notes: A Mediterranean-style tuna salad, much lighter than mayo-based versions.

name: Salmon Avocado Salad
preparation: Flake salmon into large chunks.
Slice avocado.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate.
Top with salmon, avocado, tomatoes, cucumber, and onion.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing and garnish with dill.
notes: Rich in omega-3 fatty acids from both salmon and avocado.

name: Shrimp Citrus Salad
preparation: Cook shrimp and let cool.
Segment orange and grapefruit over a bowl to catch juice.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate.
Top with shrimp, citrus segments, and red onion.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, citrus juice, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing and garnish with mint.
notes: A light, elegant salad with sweet citrus and tender shrimp.

name: Roasted Beet Goat Cheese Salad
preparation: Wrap beets in foil and roast at 200°C for 45 minutes. Cool, peel, and slice.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate.
Top with roasted beet slices.
Crumble goat cheese and sprinkle walnuts.
Whisk olive oil, balsamic vinegar, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing and serve.
notes: An elegant salad where earthy beets meet tangy goat cheese.

name: Pear Walnut Salad
preparation: Slice pear thinly.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate.
Add pear slices and toasted walnuts.
Crumble blue cheese on top.
Whisk olive oil, vinegar, honey, and salt.
Drizzle dressing and serve.
notes: Sweet pear, crunchy walnuts, and pungent blue cheese in perfect harmony.

name: Apple Pecan Salad
preparation: Thinly slice apple.
Arrange greens and top with apple slices.
Add pecans, cranberries, and goat cheese.
Whisk olive oil, vinegar, mustard, and salt.
Drizzle dressing over the salad.
Toss gently and serve.
notes: A fall-inspired salad with apple, pecans, and cranberries.

name: Broccoli Slaw
preparation: Shred broccoli stalks, carrot, and red cabbage.
Combine with raisins and sunflower seeds.
Whisk Greek yogurt, vinegar, honey, and salt for dressing.
Pour dressing over the slaw.
Toss well to coat.
Refrigerate 15 minutes before serving.
notes: A crunchy slaw using broccoli stalks that often go to waste.

name: Coleslaw Light
preparation: Shred green cabbage, red cabbage, and carrot.
Whisk Greek yogurt, vinegar, honey, salt, pepper, and celery seed.
Pour dressing over the shredded vegetables.
Toss well to coat evenly.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Serve chilled as a side dish.
notes: A lighter coleslaw using yogurt instead of heavy mayonnaise.

name: Asian Slaw
preparation: Shred napa cabbage, julienne carrot and bell pepper.
Combine vegetables with edamame.
Whisk sesame oil, rice vinegar, soy sauce, and honey.
Pour dressing over slaw and toss.
Sprinkle sesame seeds and green onion.
Serve chilled.
notes: A crispy Asian-inspired slaw with a nutty sesame dressing.

name: Chickpea Mediterranean Salad
preparation: Combine chickpeas, tomatoes, cucumber, onion, and olives.
Add crumbled feta cheese.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, and salt.
Pour dressing and toss.
Let sit for 10 minutes for flavors to meld.
Serve at room temperature.
notes: A protein-rich Mediterranean chickpea salad with feta and olives.

name: White Bean Salad
preparation: Combine white beans, tomatoes, onion, and celery.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing over the salad.
Add parsley and toss gently.
Let rest for 15 minutes.
Serve at room temperature.
notes: Creamy white beans make this salad filling and satisfying.

name: Lentil Salad French
preparation: Cook lentils until tender but firm. Drain and cool.
Finely dice carrot, celery, and onion.
Combine lentils with vegetables.
Whisk mustard, vinegar, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing and toss.
Garnish with parsley. Serve at room temperature.
notes: French Puy lentils hold their shape perfectly in salads.

name: Avocado Tomato Salad
preparation: Cube avocado and dice tomatoes.
Finely dice red onion.
Combine avocado, tomato, and onion.
Add lime juice immediately to prevent browning.
Drizzle olive oil, add salt and pepper.
Garnish with cilantro and serve immediately.
notes: A simple, fresh salad that's essentially a chunky guacamole.

name: Avocado Mango Salad
preparation: Cube avocado and mango.
Dice red onion and chop chilli.
Combine avocado, mango, onion, and chilli.
Add lime juice, salt, and pepper.
Toss gently.
Garnish with cilantro. A tropical delight.
notes: Creamy avocado meets sweet mango in this tropical combination.

name: Guacamole Salad Bowl
preparation: Mash half the avocado, cube the other half.
Combine mashed avocado with tomato, onion, and green chilli.
Add lime juice, salt, and cumin.
Place chopped lettuce in a bowl.
Top with guacamole mixture and avocado cubes.
Sprinkle tortilla chips and cilantro.
notes: A deconstructed guacamole served as a salad bowl.

name: Cucumber Dill Salad
preparation: Thinly slice cucumber.
Whisk Greek yogurt, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Pour dressing over cucumber slices.
Add fresh dill and toss gently.
Refrigerate for 15 minutes.
Serve chilled as a refreshing side.
notes: A Scandinavian-inspired cucumber salad with creamy dill dressing.

name: Tomato Basil Mozzarella
preparation: Slice tomatoes and mozzarella into equal rounds.
Arrange alternating slices on a plate.
Tuck basil leaves between slices.
Drizzle olive oil and balsamic glaze.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve at room temperature.
notes: A variation of Caprese with balsamic glaze for extra sweetness.

name: Watermelon Feta Salad
preparation: Cube watermelon into bite-sized pieces.
Combine with sliced red onion and mint.
Crumble feta over the top.
Drizzle olive oil and lime juice.
Season with salt and pepper.
Toss gently and serve immediately.
notes: Sweet watermelon and salty feta create an irresistible contrast.

name: Grilled Chicken Caesar
preparation: Season and grill chicken breast. Let rest, then slice.
Chop romaine lettuce.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, mustard, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Toss lettuce with dressing.
Top with sliced chicken, Parmesan, and croutons.
Serve immediately.
notes: The classic Caesar with grilled chicken for a complete meal.

name: Turkey Cranberry Salad
preparation: Slice cooked turkey breast.
Arrange mixed greens in a bowl.
Add turkey, cranberries, walnuts, and apple slices.
Whisk olive oil, vinegar, honey, and salt.
Drizzle dressing over the salad.
Toss gently and serve.
notes: A salad that captures the flavors of a Thanksgiving plate.

name: Chicken Apple Salad
preparation: Dice grilled chicken and apple.
Slice celery.
Arrange greens in a bowl.
Top with chicken, apple, celery, and walnuts.
Whisk yogurt, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing and toss gently.
notes: Crisp apple adds a sweet crunch to this protein-rich chicken salad.

name: Pasta Salad Light
preparation: Cook pasta al dente, drain and rinse with cold water.
Combine pasta with tomatoes, cucumber, bell pepper, and olives.
Whisk olive oil, vinegar, salt, and Italian seasoning.
Pour dressing and toss well.
Add fresh basil.
