Refrigerate 20 minutes and serve chilled.
notes: A lighter pasta salad using whole wheat pasta and vinaigrette.

name: Orzo Salad Mediterranean
preparation: Cook orzo, drain and cool.
Combine with tomatoes, cucumber, onion, and olives.
Add feta cheese.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, and salt.
Pour dressing and toss.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.
notes: Orzo's rice-like shape makes it perfect for Mediterranean salads.

name: Tortellini Salad Light
preparation: Cook tortellini al dente, drain and cool.
Combine with tomatoes, cucumber, and bell pepper.
Whisk olive oil, balsamic vinegar, salt, pepper, and Italian seasoning.
Pour dressing and toss.
Add fresh basil.
Serve chilled.
notes: Cheese-filled tortellini makes this pasta salad extra satisfying.

name: Edamame Corn Salad
preparation: Cook edamame and corn if not pre-cooked. Cool.
Combine edamame, corn, bell pepper, and onion.
Whisk sesame oil, rice vinegar, soy sauce, and honey.
Pour dressing and toss.
Sprinkle sesame seeds.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.
notes: A protein-packed Asian-inspired salad with edamame and corn.

name: Seaweed Salad
preparation: Soak dried wakame in cold water for 5-10 minutes until expanded.
Drain and squeeze out excess water.
Whisk sesame oil, rice vinegar, soy sauce, sugar, and ginger.
Toss seaweed with the dressing.
Sprinkle sesame seeds, red pepper flakes, and green onion.
Serve chilled.
notes: A Japanese-style seaweed salad rich in iodine and minerals.

name: Tofu Asian Salad
preparation: Cube tofu and pan-fry until golden and crispy.
Julienne carrot and cucumber, shred cabbage.
Arrange greens in a bowl with vegetables.
Top with crispy tofu.
Whisk sesame oil, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and honey.
Drizzle dressing, sprinkle sesame seeds, and serve.
notes: Crispy pan-fried tofu adds protein and texture to this Asian salad.

name: Fig Prosciutto Salad Light
preparation: Quarter fresh figs.
Tear prosciutto into pieces.
Arrange arugula on a plate.
Top with figs, prosciutto, goat cheese, and walnuts.
Drizzle olive oil and balsamic vinegar.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately.
notes: An elegant salad with sweet figs and salty prosciutto.

name: Cantaloupe Prosciutto Salad
preparation: Cube cantaloupe.
Tear prosciutto into pieces.
Arrange arugula on a plate.
Top with cantaloupe and prosciutto.
Drizzle olive oil and lime juice.
Garnish with mint, salt, and pepper. Serve immediately.
notes: The classic Italian pairing of sweet melon and salty prosciutto.

name: Sweet Potato Kale Salad
preparation: Roast sweet potato cubes at 200°C for 25 minutes. Cool.
Chop kale and massage with a little olive oil for 2 minutes.
Combine kale, sweet potato, cranberries, and pumpkin seeds.
Whisk olive oil, vinegar, maple syrup, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing and toss.
Serve at room temperature.
notes: A hearty fall salad with roasted sweet potato and massaged kale.

name: Butternut Squash Salad
ingredients: 1.5 cups butternut squash, roasted and cubed
preparation: Roast butternut squash cubes at 200°C for 25-30 minutes. Cool.
Arrange mixed greens in a bowl.
Top with roasted squash, cranberries, pecans, and goat cheese.
Whisk olive oil, balsamic vinegar, salt, and cinnamon.
Drizzle dressing.
Toss gently and serve.
notes: Roasted butternut squash adds a sweet, caramelized element.

name: Roasted Vegetable Salad
preparation: Toss zucchini, bell pepper, tomatoes, and onion with olive oil, salt, pepper, and herbs.
Roast at 200°C for 20-25 minutes.
Let cool slightly.
Arrange mixed greens in a bowl.
Top with roasted vegetables.
Drizzle balsamic vinegar and serve warm.
notes: Roasting concentrates vegetable flavors for a richer salad.

name: Grain Bowl Salad
preparation: Combine cooked brown rice and quinoa in a bowl.
Add roasted sweet potato, chickpeas, cucumber, and avocado.
Whisk tahini with lemon juice, salt, and 1 tbsp water.
Drizzle tahini dressing over the bowl.
Garnish with cilantro.
Serve as a complete, balanced meal.
notes: A nourishing grain bowl with balanced macros for a complete meal.

name: Farro Salad
preparation: Cook farro until tender with a slight chew. Drain and cool.
Combine with tomatoes, cucumber, and onion.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing and toss.
Add fresh basil.
Serve at room temperature.
notes: Farro has a nutty, chewy texture that holds up well in salads.

name: Barley Salad
preparation: Cook barley until tender. Drain and cool.
Dice cucumber, bell pepper, and onion.
Combine barley with vegetables, parsley, and mint.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and cumin.
Pour dressing and toss well.
Serve at room temperature or chilled.
notes: Pearl barley is high in soluble fiber that helps lower cholesterol.

name: Citrus Fennel Salad
preparation: Thinly slice fennel bulb using a mandoline or sharp knife.
Segment orange over a bowl to catch juice.
Combine fennel, orange segments, and red onion.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, orange juice, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing and toss.
Garnish with parsley and serve.
notes: Fennel has a delicate anise flavor that pairs beautifully with citrus.

name: Orange Almond Salad
preparation: Segment oranges.
Arrange mixed greens in a bowl.
Top with orange segments, almonds, and red onion.
Whisk olive oil, vinegar, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing.
Toss gently and serve.
notes: A bright, refreshing salad with sweet oranges and crunchy almonds.

name: Grapefruit Avocado Salad
preparation: Segment grapefruit and slice avocado.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate.
Top with grapefruit segments and avocado slices.
Add red onion.
Whisk olive oil, honey, lime juice, and salt.
Drizzle dressing and serve immediately.
notes: Bitter grapefruit and creamy avocado create a sophisticated pairing.

name: Zucchini Ribbon Salad
ingredients: 2 medium zucchini, ribboned with a peeler
preparation: Use a vegetable peeler to create thin zucchini ribbons.
Arrange ribbons on a plate.
Top with cherry tomatoes, Parmesan shavings, and pine nuts.
Drizzle olive oil and lemon juice.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with basil. A beautiful raw salad.
notes: Zucchini ribbons make an elegant, low-carb pasta alternative.

name: Shaved Brussels Sprouts Salad
preparation: Trim Brussels sprouts and shave thinly with a mandoline or knife.
Combine with almonds and cranberries.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, mustard, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing over the shaved sprouts.
Toss well and let sit 10 minutes to soften slightly.
Top with shaved Parmesan and serve.
notes: Raw shaved Brussels sprouts are nutty and tender when dressed.

name: Raw Cauliflower Salad
preparation: Finely chop cauliflower into rice-sized pieces.
Dice bell pepper and cucumber.
Combine cauliflower, bell pepper, cucumber, raisins, and almonds.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, cumin, and salt.
Pour dressing and toss well.
Garnish with parsley and serve.
notes: Raw cauliflower 'rice' makes a light, low-carb salad base.

name: Antipasto Salad Light
preparation: Chop romaine lettuce.
Combine with tomatoes, artichoke hearts, roasted pepper, and olives.
Add mozzarella cubes.
Whisk olive oil, vinegar, Italian seasoning, and salt.
Pour dressing and toss.
Serve as a lighter version of Italian antipasto.
notes: All the flavors of an antipasto platter in salad form.

name: Garden Fresh Salad
preparation: Wash and tear mixed lettuce.
Halve tomatoes, slice cucumber and radish, shred carrot.
Combine all vegetables in a bowl.
Add sunflower seeds.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing, toss, and serve fresh.
notes: A classic garden salad with crisp, fresh vegetables.

name: Rainbow Salad
preparation: Prepare each vegetable separately for distinct colors.
Arrange in rainbow rows or sections on a plate.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing over the rainbow arrangement.
Toss gently before eating.
A feast for the eyes and the body.
notes: Each color represents different phytonutrients for maximum health benefits.

name: Power Green Salad
preparation: Combine spinach, kale, and mixed greens.
Add blanched broccoli and edamame.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing and massage into kale for 1 minute.
Add pumpkin seeds.
Serve as a nutrient-dense power salad.
notes: Packed with iron, calcium, and protein from multiple green sources.

name: Superfood Salad
preparation: Chop kale and massage with a little olive oil.
Add cooked quinoa.
Top with blueberries, pomegranate, pumpkin seeds, and goji berries.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, honey, and salt.
Drizzle dressing and toss.
Serve as the ultimate nutrient-dense salad.
notes: Every ingredient is a certified superfood, packed with antioxidants.

name: Detox Salad Bowl
preparation: Chop kale, shred cabbage, grate carrot and beetroot.
Add broccoli florets.
Whisk lemon juice, olive oil, vinegar, turmeric, salt, and ginger.
Pour dressing over vegetables.
Toss well, massaging dressing into kale.
Serve as a cleansing, fiber-rich detox bowl.
notes: A cleansing salad rich in fiber, antioxidants, and anti-inflammatory ingredients.

name: Berry Spinach Salad
preparation: Wash baby spinach and place in a bowl.
Add blueberries, raspberries, and sliced strawberries.
Sprinkle slivered almonds.
Whisk olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, and salt.
Drizzle dressing over the salad.
Toss gently and serve immediately.
notes: Triple berry salad loaded with antioxidants and vitamins.

name: Tropical Fruit Salad
preparation: Dice mango, pineapple, and papaya. Slice kiwi.
Combine all fruits in a bowl.
Drizzle lime juice and honey.
Sprinkle cardamom powder and coconut flakes.
Toss gently.
Serve chilled for a tropical treat.
notes: A tropical paradise in a bowl, naturally sweet and refreshing.

name: Ambrosia Salad Light
preparation: Combine pineapple, oranges, coconut, grapes, and strawberries.
Mix Greek yogurt with honey.
Fold fruit into the yogurt mixture.
Add mini marshmallows.
Gently stir to combine.
Chill for 30 minutes before serving.
notes: A lighter ambrosia using Greek yogurt instead of whipped cream.

name: Grilled Peach Salad
preparation: Halve peaches and grill cut-side down for 2-3 minutes.
Let cool slightly and slice.
Arrange arugula on a plate.
Top with grilled peach slices, goat cheese, and walnuts.
Drizzle olive oil and balsamic vinegar.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve warm.
notes: Grilling caramelizes the peach and intensifies its sweetness.

name: Strawberry Goat Cheese Salad
preparation: Arrange mixed greens in a bowl.
Add sliced strawberries and crumbled goat cheese.
Sprinkle toasted pecans.
Whisk olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing over the salad.
Toss gently and serve immediately.
notes: A classic pairing of sweet strawberries and tangy goat cheese.

name: Blueberry Almond Salad
preparation: Arrange mixed greens in a bowl.
Add blueberries, toasted almonds, and feta cheese.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, honey, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing over the salad.
Toss gently.
Serve immediately for peak freshness.
notes: Blueberries are a superfood packed with antioxidants.
